description: Festech extension registry. Defines the host-specific primitives that festech.live layers on top of the SSSS core (registry/core.json). Lifted from festech's docs/ssss/primitive-registry.json and assigned a portability class (§5.5) so a festech workspace can be exported as a backup/template/sale bundle. Core primitives (assistant, workflow, rule, security_role, skill, model, memory, task, conversation, run, conflict, page, migration, release) are NOT redefined here — they are governed by core.json. An extension MUST NOT redefine a core type.
conformance: A festech-conformant host MUST resolve portability for these primitives from this registry. The `resource_bound` primitives MUST ship only a requirement declaration (never the seller's bound value) in a template/sale export; the `tenant_private` primitives MUST be dropped from template/sale entirely (§5.5).

domain.notes: The seller's domain is NEVER transferred. In template/sale the bundle declares 'this business needs a domain'; the buyer binds their own at provision (§16).

integration_connection.notes: Carries credentials/tokens to a third-party provider — the seller's connection value MUST be stripped; the buyer supplies their own at provision.

extension.notes: Which extensions a workspace has installed is part of the business model and ships in a sale.

media_asset.notes: Brand/marketing assets are structural. An asset holding customer-uploaded content SHOULD override with x_portability: tenant_private.

knowledge.notes: The curated knowledge base is a core sellable asset of the business model.

knowledge_source.notes: A source backing a structural knowledge base. A source ingesting private customer documents SHOULD override with x_portability: tenant_private.

settings.notes: Workspace settings configure the business model (structural). Account-scoped settings SHOULD override with x_portability: tenant_private.

system_settings.notes: Host/operator-level configuration; never transfers to a buyer.
